title: 〜ため（に）
description: 〜ため（に）marks a purpose ("in order to"), a beneficiary ("for the sake of"), or a cause ("because of / due to") depending on context. It is more formal than 〜から・〜ので and is common in written Japanese.

content.text: In its most common use, 〜ため（に）expresses purpose or benefit. Attach ために to a dictionary-form verb to mean "in order to" do it, or to a noun with の to mean "for the sake of" that person or thing. 痩（や）せる → 痩（や）せるために ("in order to lose weight"); 子供（こども）→ 子供（こども）のために ("for the children").

content.japanese: dictionary form ＋ ために　／　noun ＋ の ＋ ために　（in order to／for the sake of）
痩（や）せるために毎朝（まいあさ）走（はし）っています。
content.english: I run every morning in order to lose weight.

content.japanese: マディちゃんのためにクッキーを買（か）いました。
イベントのために予定（よてい）を作（つく）っています。
content.english: I'm putting together a schedule for the event.

content.japanese: お客様（きゃくさま）に来（き）てもらうために何（なに）をしますか。
content.english: What will you do to get the customers to come?

content.text: 〜ため also states a cause — "because of" or "due to." In this meaning に is usually dropped, and the tone is formal and objective, which makes it the standard choice for announcements, news, and signs. Attach it to a noun with の or to a plain-form clause.

content.japanese: noun ＋ の ＋ ため　／　plain form ＋ ため　（because of／due to）
台風（たいふう）のため、電車（でんしゃ）が止（と）まっています。
content.english: Due to the typhoon, the trains are stopped.

content.japanese: スモッグがひどいため、マスクをつけます。
content.english: Because the smog is bad, I wear a mask.

content.text: ため vs から・ので

All three give a reason, but 〜ため is the most formal and impersonal — it fits written and public language (工事（こうじ）のため通行止（つうこうど）め, "closed due to construction"). 〜から is subjective and assertive ("because I say so"), and 〜ので is softer and more polite for everyday speech. Note that only the cause meaning of ため competes with these; when ために means "in order to," it is about purpose, not reason, so it can't be swapped for から・ので.

examples.japanese: dictionary form ＋ ために　／　noun ＋ の ＋ ために　（in order to／for the sake of）
痩（や）せるために毎朝（まいあさ）走（はし）っています。
examples.english: I run every morning in order to lose weight.

examples.japanese: マディちゃんのためにクッキーを買（か）いました。
イベントのために予定（よてい）を作（つく）っています。
examples.english: I'm putting together a schedule for the event.

examples.japanese: お客様（きゃくさま）に来（き）てもらうために何（なに）をしますか。
examples.english: What will you do to get the customers to come?

examples.japanese: noun ＋ の ＋ ため　／　plain form ＋ ため　（because of／due to）
台風（たいふう）のため、電車（でんしゃ）が止（と）まっています。
examples.english: Due to the typhoon, the trains are stopped.

examples.japanese: スモッグがひどいため、マスクをつけます。
examples.english: Because the smog is bad, I wear a mask.

headings.text: ため vs から・ので
